blockingIssues.description: The FEN parser accepts castling-right strings in arbitrary order, but the serializer always rewrites them in canonical `KQkq` order, so some accepted inputs do not round-trip identically.
`eval_evaluate()` returns a white-centric score while the negamax search consumes it as a side-to-move static eval, producing the wrong sign on black-to-move nodes.
The mate-in-1 regression coverage does not satisfy `VAL-SEARCH-001` because it omits the required back-rank and smothered-mate motifs.
Late-move reductions are gated behind `depth >= 5`, so the requested `depth >= 3` behavior is missing.
Aspiration windows start too late and always fall back to a full-window re-search after the widened retry, which does not match the requested retry flow.
The WASM build uses only the tiny embedded fallback opening book and never consumes the bundled `engine/book/rodent.bin` at runtime.
The public `set_position(fen)` API reparses into a fresh `Position`, resetting repetition history so threefold detection does not work in the documented FEN-only frontend flow.
`pnpm test` depends on ignored `public/engine/engine.js` and `public/engine/engine.wasm` artifacts, so the package-level test entrypoint is not self-contained on a clean checkout.

The worker bootstrap dynamically imports `/engine/engine.js`, but the Emscripten build still emits CommonJS/AMD-style glue rather than an ES module, so the browser module worker cannot construct the engine.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/engine-legality.md` to note that make/unmake integrity must also restore `pawn_hash`, `pst_mg`, and `pst_eg` exactly.
Updated `.factory/library/engine-book-api.md` to document that the WASM build currently uses the embedded fallback book and that FEN-only `set_position()` flows reset repetition history.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update engine-worker instructions to use `engine/src/` and `engine/tests/`, the current gcc command from `.factory/services.yaml`, and milestone-scoped expectations instead of assuming every engine task includes perft or root-level `tests/` paths.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The same stale path/command mismatch was reported by reviews for engine-board-representation, engine-move-generation, engine-special-moves-legality, engine-perft-testing, engine-evaluation, engine-search, engine-pruning-extensions, and engine-book-draw-time.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align the WASM integration guidance with the actual runtime requirements: either emit an ES-module Emscripten bundle for module-worker `import()`, or document a classic-worker / `importScripts()` loading path instead of mixing `MODULARIZE` output with browser `import()`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: wasm-worker-api failed scrutiny because the worker imports `/engine/engine.js` as a module even though `scripts/build-engine.sh` still emits CommonJS/AMD-style glue; wasm-build-integration also reported stale integration-worker layout guidance.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Normalize the web-port expectation to 3001 everywhere, including feature verification steps, so workers and reviewers are not split between historical port 3000 references and the current reserved-port policy.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The wasm-build-integration review found `features.json` still pointing validation to port 3000 while `AGENTS.md` and `.factory/services.yaml` require port 3001.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` must reflect the actual transcript order, and require deviations when implementation precedes test creation on TDD-scoped skills.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The engine-search review found `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` even though the transcript shows search implementation files were created before `engine/tests/test_search.c`.

rejectedObservations.observation: `.factory/library/architecture.md` described move generation as including legality filtering and special moves before the milestone finished.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected as ambiguous/stale: the current integrated engine architecture does include those responsibilities, so the earlier staged-feature mismatch is no longer a reliable shared-state correction target.

rejectedObservations.observation: Mission state should document that frontend package scripts may be absent during engine-only milestones.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected as already addressed in the current `.factory/services.yaml`, which now guards missing package scripts for lint/typecheck/test execution.

rejectedObservations.observation: The shared library should contain a canned corpus of search-regression FENs.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected as a good future improvement but not a factual inconsistency discovered in current shared state.
